highlights.title: Add Omnipod (Omni) pump support
changes: Trio adds the OmnipodKit submodule so Omnipod (Omni) pump support is included.
A new "All Omnipod Types" option appears in pump selection and setup screens.
Trio records Omnipod pod details such as expiration time and reservoir level for monitoring.
The pump setup flow now includes an Omni setup option to start pairing and configuring Omnipod pumps.

highlights.title: OmnipodKit support and telemetry fixes
changes: A new "All Omnipod Types" pump option is available in the Add Pump flow.
If you choose the new option, the pod type (Eros vs DASH) is selected during pod activation.
Existing OmniKit and OmniBLE users see no change unless they opt in to the new driver.
Telemetry background sends are now correctly limited to once per day, and privacy/diagnostics text has been clarified.
A rare spurious "Bolus Failed" notification after a manual bolus has been fixed.

sources.title: Add OmnipodKit submodule, support use of OmnipodKit as PM
Fix Telemetry daily send for continous pings

highlights.title: Stop false 'Bolus failed' alerts after manual bolus
changes: Manual boluses that the pump delivered won't trigger a false "Bolus failed" alert when the algorithm update fails.
Algorithm refresh errors after a manual bolus are logged as warnings instead of raising a pump error notification.
You still receive the normal bolus success confirmation when a manual bolus completes.

sources.title: nightscout/Trio#1151: Fix spurious 'Bolus failed' notification after manual bolus
Fix spurious 'Bolus failed' notification after manual bolus

highlights.title: OmnipodKit updated with translations
changes: The app now includes an updated OmnipodKit component.
Some OmnipodKit interface strings were translated.
New issue templates were added to the OmnipodKit repository.

sources.title: update OmnipodKit:  translate strings, add Issue Templates

items.title: OmnipodKit support plus telemetry and security fixes
changes: A new "All Omnipod Types" option is available when adding a pump to support Omnipod pods.
Pod model (Eros vs. DASH) is selected when you activate a pod, not when adding the pump.
Existing OmniKit and OmniBLE users see no change unless they explicitly switch to the new driver.
Background telemetry pings are now correctly limited to once per day to stop repeated sends.
Build/release tooling dependencies were updated for security; this does not change shipped app behavior.

sources.title: nightscout/Trio#1174: Trio v0.8.1 patch release – sync to dev

items.title: Telemetry: standardized buildDate and added locale/timeZone
changes: Telemetry now records the device system locale and time zone.
Telemetry's buildDate is now emitted as YYYY-MM-DD in UTC instead of a localized text string.
Telemetry now includes an appName field set to Trio to help distinguish forks.
The privacy policy was updated to document the added locale and time zone fields and the policy date.

sources.title: nightscout/Trio#1172: Update telemetry payload: fix buildDate format, add locale/TZ/appName

items.title: Fix Telemetry app name reporting
changes: Telemetry now includes the correct app name when sending data.
Telemetry reporting is more reliable after this fix.

includedPullRequests.title: Fix spurious 'Bolus failed' notification after manual bolus
includedPullRequests.body:
fixes #1150 
The post-bolus determineBasalSync() call was inside the bolus do/catch, so any algorithm error (e.g. 'Not enough glucose data' during CGM warm-up) was misreported as a bolus failure even though the pump had already delivered the bolus successfully. Wrap the algorithm refresh in its own do/catch so it cannot trigger bolusDidFail or a pumpError notification.

includedPullRequests.title: Fix spurious 'Bolus failed' notification after manual bolus
includedPullRequests.body:
fixes #1150 
The post-bolus determineBasalSync() call was inside the bolus do/catch, so any algorithm error (e.g. 'Not enough glucose data' during CGM warm-up) was misreported as a bolus failure even though the pump had already delivered the bolus successfully. Wrap the algorithm refresh in its own do/catch so it cannot trigger bolusDidFail or a pumpError notification.

includedPullRequests.title: Trio v0.8.1 patch release
includedPullRequests.body:
## Summary
Rolls three recently-landed tracks into a patch release: the new OmnipodKit pump manager and a security-driven dependency bump.

## What's Included

  1. OmnipodKit pump manager (Eros + DASH unified) — adds loopandlearn/OmnipodKit as a new submodule plus the wiring to surface it in the Add Pump flow as "All Omnipod Types". Pod type (Eros vs. DASH) is selected at activation rather than at the pump-manager level. OmniKit and OmniBLE remain in place unchanged — existing users on those drivers see no behavior change; the new driver is purely opt-in until a user explicitly selects it on next pod start.

  2. Security: fastlane 2.235.0 / jwt 3.2.0 — bumps fastlane 2.233.1 → 2.235.0, which relaxes its transitive jwt < 3 cap and lets the resolver land jwt 3.2.0+. Closes Dependabot alert #14 (ruby-jwt empty-key HMAC bypass, sibling of CVE-2026-44351).
  CI/release tooling only, no shipped-app code surface change. 

  3. Fix Telemetry Daily Sends — continuous background pings were not being correctly throttled to once per day. The daily send logic is now enforced properly. 

It also includes small fixes added recently to dev:
* Fix Spurious "Bolus Failed" Notification After Manual Bolus (https://github.com/nightscout/Trio/pull/1151)
* Telemetry Payload Improvements (#1172)

includedPullRequests.title: Update telemetry payload: fix buildDate format, add locale/TZ/appName
includedPullRequests.body:
Fixes a few issues identified with the Trio telemetry payloads:

* On non-English locales, the buildDate was improperly formatted. e.g., a builder on a French language-default OS would emit `lun. mai 2`. Fix: force capture-build-details to POSIX default locale (english).
* Parse the timestamp and emit as YYYY-MM-DD instead of “Tue May 26” format
* Add iOS system locale + timezone to telemetry, with the above bug as motivation for why we should track this. Plus, this will be beneficial for users when managing translations.
* Add appName to telemetry -- hardcoded as "Trio", but can be overridden for forks

includedPullRequests.title: Update telemetry payload: fix buildDate format, add locale/TZ/appName
includedPullRequests.body:

* Parse the timestamp and emit as YYYY-MM-DD instead of “Tue May 26” format
* Add iOS system locale + timezone to telemetry, with the above bug as motivation for why we should track this. Plus, this will be beneficial for users when managing translations.
* Add appName to telemetry -- hardcoded as "Trio", but can be overridden for forks
